query:
You are a SaaS pricing analyst. For each of the 10 companies below, extract their CURRENT pricing structure from their public pricing pages ({domain}/pricing, {domain}/plans), G2, Capterra, and reverse-engineering posts.

Companies:
  1. name="Nalo", website="https://nalo.fr"
  2. name="Linxo", website="https://linxo.com"
  3. name="Grisbee", website="https://grisbee.com"
  4. name="Horiz.io", website="https://horiz.io"
  5. name="Sharesight", website="https://sharesight.com"
  6. name="InvMon", website="https://invmon.com"
  7. name="Wealthfolio", website="https://ealthfolio.app"
  8. name="Quicken", website="https://quicken.com"
  9. name="Merlin", website="https://merlin.com"
  10. name="Sure", website="https://sure.com"

For EACH company extract:
- pricing_model_kind: one of Freemium, Subscription, Usage-based, Enterprise, Hybrid
- free_plan: true/false
- starts_at_usd: lowest paid tier monthly USD (0 if free plan only, null if Enterprise-only / contact sales)
- mention: short UI tagline e.g. 'Starts at $20/user/mo' or 'Contact sales' or 'Free + paid plans from $8/mo'
- tiers: array of {name, price_monthly_usd, price_annual_usd, features (3-6 strings), target}. Include ALL public tiers (e.g. Free, Pro, Business, Enterprise). For Enterprise tiers without public price, set price_monthly_usd=null and target='Enterprise'.
- recent_changes: any pricing change in last 12 months (date + source). Leave blank if none.

Omit any field you cannot verify — do NOT invent prices. Return one object per company in the SAME order as the input list. Preserve company names EXACTLY.
answer:
{"executive_summary": "- **Freemium Dominance**: 6 of 10 companies (Linxo, Grisbee, Sharesight, InvMon, Wealthfolio, Merlin AI) offer a free tier, making freemium the dominant model in investment/finance SaaS -> Consider free-tier acquisition funnels as the baseline competitive expectation in this vertical.
- **AUM-Based Pricing Outlier**: Nalo charges percentage-of-AUM fees (0.85%-1.65%/yr) rather than flat subscriptions, aligning cost with portfolio size -> Usage-based models suit robo-advisory products where value scales with assets.
- **Open-Source + Optional Paid Sync**: Wealthfolio combines a free local-first open-source app with a 4-tier Connect subscription ($3.99-$24.99/mo) for cloud/brokerage features -> Hybrid open-source/commercial models can monetize convenience while preserving privacy as a differentiator.
- **One-Time Purchase Option**: InvMon uniquely offers a $199 lifetime license alongside monthly subscriptions ($12/mo) and a free tier -> Lifetime deals reduce churn risk for privacy-conscious desktop users who resist recurring billing.
- **Enterprise-Only InsurTech**: Sure (sureapp.com) and Horiz.io PRO have no public pricing, requiring sales contact -> B2B insurance and professional real-estate tools default to opaque pricing, limiting comparability.
- **Promotional Pricing Distortion**: Quicken runs a persistent "up to 50% off" promotion (Simplifi at $2.99/mo vs. regular ~$3.99/mo), making true baseline pricing harder to ascertain from the official site alone -> Cross-reference G2/Capterra for non-promotional rates.
- **Telecom Domain Mismatch**: sure.com resolves to a Channel Islands telecom (mobile/broadband), not the insurtech SaaS at sureapp.com -> Domain verification is critical; the listed URL may not match the intended product.
- **Merlin Domain Mismatch**: merlin.com does not resolve to a SaaS product; Merlin AI lives at getmerlin.in -> Confirming product identity from the specified domain alone can yield irrelevant or null results.
- **Grisbee Zero-Cost Model**: Grisbee is entirely free for end users, monetized through financial-institution commissions, making it the only truly zero-cost platform -> Commission-based models eliminate user price sensitivity but create potential conflicts of interest.
- **Sharesight Tiered Portfolios**: Sharesight scales by portfolio count (1 free, up to 10 on Premium at $23.25/mo) and holdings limits, with a separate per-portfolio Business tier ($13.50/portfolio/mo) -> Portfolio-count metering aligns pricing with professional use intensity.
- **Horiz.io Annual Discount**: Annual billing at 8.25 EUR/mo represents a 58% discount vs. monthly at 19.99 EUR/mo -> Strong annual discounting incentivizes commitment and reduces churn in subscription-only products.
- **InvMon Renewal Drop**: InvMon's subscription drops from $119/yr to $59/yr after year 2, rewarding retention -> Declining renewal pricing is rare but effective for long-term lock-in.

---", "1_nalo": "| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **pricing_model_kind** | Usage-based |
| **free_plan** | false |
| **starts_at_usd** | null |
| **mention** | Management fees from 0.85%/yr (AUM-based) |

**tiers:**

| Tier | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | features | target |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Nalo Patrimoine (Assurance-vie) | null (AUM-based) | null (AUM-based) | Max 1.65%/yr all-in fee; UC management 1.40%/yr (0.90% Nalo + 0.50% insurer); Euro Fund 0.85%/yr; Avg ETF support 0.25%; 0 EUR entry/versement/arbitrage fees | Individual investors |
| Nalo PER (Plan epargne retraite) | null (AUM-based) | null (AUM-based) | Max 1.60%/yr all-in fee; UC management 1.35%/yr (0.90% Nalo + 0.45% insurer); Euro Fund 0.85%/yr; Avg ETF support 0.25%; 0 EUR entry/versement/arbitrage fees | Retirement savers |

Nalo is not a traditional SaaS subscription product. It is a robo-advisory investment platform (assurance-vie and PER) where fees are deducted quarterly as a percentage of assets under management. There is no flat monthly or annual subscription fee. The "Nalo Ambassadeur" referral program provides three months of fee exemptions. Fees are generally below those of traditional banking networks ([1_nalo[0]] [4]).

Linxo is a French personal finance management app (owned by Credit Agricole) with 2.8M+ users. The Premium tier adds forecasting and unlimited search/categorization. Sharesight is an online portfolio tracker serving 500K+ investors since 2007. Pricing scales primarily by portfolio count and holdings. The Business tier is metered per portfolio. Annual billing offers meaningful savings across all tiers. The last documented pricing increase took effect April 4, 2022, affecting NZ/AU markets only (Starter to $19/mo, Investor to $29/mo, Expert to $49/mo in those regions); no pricing change was found in the last 12 months for global/USD pricing. InvMon is a locally-installed portfolio management application. Its pricing model is uniquely hybrid: freemium entry, one-time lifetime purchase, and recurring subscription with a declining renewal rate ($119/yr dropping to $59/yr). The Subscription tier includes a 2-week free trial. The Pro tier is listed as "in development" with no public price. ([6_invmon[0]] [27], [6_invmon[1]] [28])

Wealthfolio is an open-source, local-first portfolio tracker. The base app is free and runs entirely on-device with no cloud dependency. The optional "Connect" subscription adds brokerage synchronization (via SnapTrade, SOC 2 Type II certified) and E2E-encrypted multi-device sync. Connect pricing was not disclosed on toolradar.com as of their June 2026 review but has since been published on the Connect page at wealthfolio.app/connect with the four tiers above. The App Store listing shows the app as "Free" with no listed in-app purchases. ([7_wealthfolio[0]] [31], [7_wealthfolio[1]] [97], [7_wealthfolio[2]] [79], [7_wealthfolio[3]] [78])

**tiers:**

| Tier | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | features | target |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Simplifi | 3.99 (promo: 2.99) | 47.88 (promo: 35.88) | Connectivity to 14,000+ financial institutions; budget tracking; custom savings goals; personalized insights; investment portfolio management; retirement modeling | Personal budgeters |
| Classic Starter | 3.99 | 47.88 | Bank/credit card sync; automatic spending categorization; simple monthly budgets; bill viewing & tracking | Basic desktop users |
| Classic Deluxe | 5.99 | 71.88 | All Starter features; debt management; retirement planning; multiple budgets | Personal finance desktop users |

| Classic Business & Personal | 10.99 | 131.88 | All Premier features; business/rental/personal finance; tax optimization; P&L, cash flow, tax schedule reports | Desktop power users with rental properties |

Quicken offers both cloud (Simplifi, Business & Personal) and desktop (Classic) products, all billed annually with no free tier. The official site currently promotes "up to 50% off for a limited time" (Simplifi at $2.99/mo, Business & Personal at $3.99/mo). Regular prices above are derived from Capterra and the official site. A 30-day money-back guarantee applies. LifeHub (document organization) is available as a bundle add-on without a standalone listed price. Community discussions document repeated annual price increases (2024: Classic from $6.99 to $7.99/mo; 2025: further increases reported). ([8_quicken[0]] [89], [8_quicken[1]] [93], [8_quicken[2]] [87], [8_quicken[3]] [72])

**recent_changes:** Quicken has implemented recurring annual price increases. Reddit users reported a Classic subscription increase from $6.99/mo ($83.88/yr) to $7.99/mo ($95.88/yr) effective January 2024. Community posts in 2025 describe further increases (some users reporting prices doubling from 2024 levels). Source: [8_quicken[3]] [72], [8_quicken[4]] [73].

**Note:** The specified domain merlin.com does not resolve to a SaaS product with public pricing. Merlin AI (the AI assistant product) operates at getmerlin.in. The pricing data above reflects Merlin AI at getmerlin.in/pricing. merlin.com appears to be unaffiliated (potential redirects to Merlin Law Group, Merlin Entertainments, or other entities). | **mention** | Contact sales |

**tiers:**

|---|---|---|---|---|
| Enterprise | null | null | AI-powered underwriting & rating; whitelabeled digital insurance platform; configurable coverages, rates & limits; policy issuance in under 1 second; auditable ledger | Insurance carriers / brands |

**Note:** The specified domain sure.com resolves to Sure (Guernsey) Limited, a Channel Islands telecommunications company offering mobile and broadband plans (from 19 GBP/mo). This is NOT the insurtech SaaS product. The insurtech "Sure" platform operates at sureapp.com and provides an AI-powered digital insurance infrastructure for carriers and brands. sureapp.com has no public pricing; all engagement is enterprise/contact-sales. ([10_sure[0]] [99], [10_sure[1]] [63], [10_sure[2]] [64])

**recent_changes:** 

---", "synthesis": "The 10 companies span four distinct pricing paradigms, each mapping to their product archetype:

| Paradigm | Companies | Key Mechanism | Price Range |
|---|---|---|---|
| AUM-based (Usage) | Nalo | Percentage of assets under management | 0.85%-1.65%/yr |
| Freemium SaaS | Linxo, Sharesight, InvMon, Wealthfolio, Merlin AI | Free tier + paid upgrade | $3.99-$24.99/mo |
| Subscription-only | Horiz.io, Quicken | No free tier; annual billing | 8.25 EUR-$10.99/mo |
| Enterprise/contact-sales | Grisbee (indirect), Sure (insurtech) | No user-facing pricing | N/A |

**Cross-cutting tensions:**

- **Free vs. paid value cliff**: Grisbee offers everything free (monetized via institutions), while Horiz.io offers nothing free (subscription-only). This creates a sharp divide in the French market where both operate - Grisbee removes all price friction for users but bears a conflict-of-interest risk, while Horiz.io captures revenue from day one but faces higher acquisition barriers.
- **One-time vs. recurring**: InvMon's $199 lifetime license directly competes with its own $12/mo subscription, creating internal cannibalization risk. The declining renewal ($59/yr after year 2) further complicates LTV modeling. No other company in this set offers a lifetime option.
- **Domain-identity mismatches**: Both Merlin (merlin.com vs. getmerlin.in) and Sure (sure.com vs. sureapp.com) have the listed domain pointing to an unrelated entity. This is a data-quality risk for any automated pricing extraction pipeline - domain verification is essential before scraping.
- **Annual discounting intensity**: Horiz.io's 58% annual discount is the most aggressive in the set, followed by Quicken's promotional 50% off and Linxo's ~44%. This suggests high churn sensitivity in investment/finance SaaS, where users may reassess subscriptions when markets underperform.
- **Privacy as a monetization lever**: Both InvMon and Wealthfolio emphasize 100% local/private data as a core free-tier feature, then charge for cloud/sync capabilities. This inverts the typical SaaS pattern where cloud is the default and privacy is the premium feature.

---"}

citations.title: Linxo Launches Real-Time Payment Card - Finovate
excerpts:
's app will work in tandem with the new payment card by categorizing and monitoring expenses, offering a view of all the user's accounts, and providing a budget forecast that predicts the user's future balance based on their current spending.
Founded in 2010 and headquartered in France, Linxo offers its 2.8 million users a personal financial management app that aggregates all of their spending information across accounts.
Some features, such as the budget forecast, are only available with Linxo Premium, a service available for $34 (€29.99) or $5 (€4.49) per month.
